name: Kings Peak
description_short: Member-owned credit union in Roosevelt, UT offering financial services to its members. NCUA-insured institution providing basic banking and lending products.
description_long:
Kings Peak Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving the Roosevelt, Utah community. The institution is federally insured by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration), providing members with deposit protection and regulatory oversight typical of legitimate credit unions. Located at 180 N 300 E in Roosevelt, UT 84066, Kings Peak has established itself as a community financial institution with local branch presence.

As a credit union, Kings Peak offers the standard suite of financial products available to its members. While the provided website content does not detail specific offerings, credit unions of this type typically provide share accounts (savings), share certificates (CDs), member loans, and various lending products. Members access these services through their physical branch location and can contact the institution directly at (435) 722-3795 for information on current products, rates, and hours of operation.

Kings Peak distinguishes itself through its credit union structure, which means it operates on a not-for-profit, member-owned basis rather than as a commercial bank seeking to maximize shareholder profits. This structural difference typically results in more favorable terms for members, including lower fees and competitive rates on loans and savings products. As a locally-rooted institution in Roosevelt, the credit union provides personalized service and community connection that larger national banks cannot replicate.

Prospective members should note that the available website information is limited, providing primarily location and contact details. Individuals interested in membership eligibility, specific product terms, rates, and full service offerings should contact Kings Peak directly by phone. The lack of comprehensive online information is typical for smaller community credit unions but may require more direct outreach to evaluate whether membership benefits align with individual financial needs.
services: In-person banking at Roosevelt branch location
Member account management and financial services
Local financial guidance and relationship banking
Standard credit union products and services
pros: NCUA-insured member deposits provide federal protection up to $250,000
Not-for-profit structure typically results in lower fees than commercial banks
Local branch presence in Roosevelt, UT provides in-person service and relationship banking
Member-owned institution means profits benefit members rather than external shareholders
Direct phone contact (435) 722-3795 available for member inquiries and support
Community-focused credit union structure allows for personalized financial guidance
Physical location at 180 N 300 E provides accessible service to Roosevelt-area residents
cons: Limited online presence and website information makes it difficult to evaluate specific products, rates, and terms without contacting the institution directly
Smaller credit union may have fewer ATMs and branches compared to national banks, limiting convenience for members outside Roosevelt
Membership eligibility may be restricted to specific employer groups, geographic areas, or communities, potentially excluding some applicants
No detailed information available about loan types, APRs, or savings product rates online
Limited digital banking capabilities information suggests potentially fewer online and mobile banking features than larger financial institutions
best_for: Roosevelt, UT residents seeking local community banking with personalized service
Individuals prioritizing member-owned, not-for-profit financial institutions over commercial banks
Members of eligible groups who value lower fees and competitive rates typical of credit unions
Savers wanting NCUA-insured deposit protection and relationship-based financial management
diagnosis: Kings Peak Credit Union is best for Roosevelt-area residents and eligible members seeking community-based, not-for-profit banking with NCUA protection and personalized service. The main caveat is that limited online information requires direct phone contact to evaluate specific products, rates, membership eligibility, and whether the institution meets individual financial needs.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
